sections.paragraph:
ἐκκολάπτω 1 aor. ἐξεκόλαψα LXX; pf. pass. ptc. ἐκκεκολαμμένος (ins: Sb 7259, 34 [95/94 b.c.]) (κολάπτω ‘peck’, then ‘engrave’, s. next entry; Thu. [1, 132, 3] et al.; SIG 543, 27; 1047, 44; Ex 36:13) to chip material off a stone surface, chisel out λίθος ὡς ἐκ πέτρας ἐ. a stone apparently chiseled out of a rock (i.e. it appeared to be a fashioned monolith) Hs 9, 9, 7.—DELG s.v. κολάπτω.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 304
